What Are JavaScript Generator Functions (function*)? The Function You Can Pause and Resume

function* is one of those syntaxes hardly anyone reaches for, but it's surprisingly powerful. It lets you define a special kind of function called a "generator function" — one you can pause and resume at will. In this post I'll walk through what generator functions are, how yield works, and the real situations where they actually come in handy.
What Is a Promise in JavaScript? And Why You Really Need to Get It

This post walks through what a Promise actually is in JavaScript, plus the Async/Await syntax that builds on top of it. We'll cover how to write a Promise, the then/catch chain, the static methods like Promise.all and Promise.race, the three Promise states, and how async/await cleans all of it up into something far easier to read.
JavaScript Core Concepts (ES6 Edition): Notes From Will Huang's Kaohsiung Class

A few of the more fun things I picked up from Will Huang's "JavaScript in Practice: Core Concepts (ES6 Edition)" class in Kaohsiung. Empty arrays that fill themselves with blanks, var that won't get overwritten, why Number is pure evil, and a handful of other JS quirks worth knowing.
var vs let in JavaScript — Plus What Happens When You Forget to Declare a Variable

Plenty of people online have covered the difference between var and let, and what happens when you skip the declaration keyword entirely. So here's my take on it too — the biggest differences between var and let, why forgetting the keyword bites you, and the one weird thing almost nobody mentions: why a let variable can't be found on window but still works.
